speaker: KING
text: Go call the Earls of Surrey and of Warwick;

speaker: KING
text: But, ere they come, bid them o’erread these letters

speaker: KING
text: And well consider of them. Make good speed.

speaker: KING
text: How many thousand of my poorest subjects

speaker: KING
text: Are at this hour asleep! O sleep, O gentle sleep,

speaker: KING
text: Nature’s soft nurse, how have I frighted thee,

speaker: KING
text: That thou no more wilt weigh my eyelids down

speaker: KING
text: And steep my senses in forgetfulness?

speaker: KING
text: Why rather, sleep, liest thou in smoky cribs,

speaker: KING
text: Upon uneasy pallets stretching thee,

speaker: KING
text: And hushed with buzzing night-flies to thy slumber,

speaker: KING
text: Than in the perfumed chambers of the great,

speaker: KING
text: Under the canopies of costly state,

speaker: KING
text: And lulled with sound of sweetest melody?

speaker: KING
text: O thou dull god, why liest thou with the vile

speaker: KING
text: In loathsome beds and leavest the kingly couch

speaker: KING
text: A watch-case or a common ’larum bell?

speaker: KING
text: Wilt thou upon the high and giddy mast

speaker: KING
text: Seal up the shipboy’s eyes and rock his brains

speaker: KING
text: In cradle of the rude imperious surge

speaker: KING
text: And in the visitation of the winds,

speaker: KING
text: Who take the ruffian billows by the top,

speaker: KING
text: Curling their monstrous heads and hanging them

speaker: KING
text: With deafing clamor in the slippery clouds

speaker: KING
text: That with the hurly death itself awakes?

speaker: KING
text: Canst thou, O partial sleep, give thy repose

speaker: KING
text: To the wet sea-boy in an hour so rude,

speaker: KING
text: And, in the calmest and most stillest night,

speaker: KING
text: With all appliances and means to boot,

speaker: KING
text: Deny it to a king? Then, happy low, lie down.

speaker: KING
text: Uneasy lies the head that wears a crown.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Many good morrows to your Majesty.

speaker: KING
text: Is it good morrow, lords?

speaker: WARWICK
text: ’Tis one o’clock, and past.

speaker: KING
text: Why then, good morrow to you all, my lords.

speaker: KING
text: Have you read o’er the letter that I sent you?

speaker: WARWICK
text: We have, my liege.

speaker: KING
text: Then you perceive the body of our kingdom

speaker: KING
text: How foul it is, what rank diseases grow,

speaker: KING
text: And with what danger near the heart of it.

speaker: WARWICK
text: It is but as a body yet distempered,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Which to his former strength may be restored

speaker: WARWICK
text: With good advice and little medicine.

speaker: WARWICK
text: My Lord Northumberland will soon be cooled.

speaker: KING
text: O God, that one might read the book of fate

speaker: KING
text: And see the revolution of the times

speaker: KING
text: Make mountains level, and the continent,

speaker: KING
text: Weary of solid firmness, melt itself

speaker: KING
text: Into the sea, and other times to see

speaker: KING
text: The beachy girdle of the ocean

speaker: KING
text: Too wide for Neptune’s hips; how chance’s mocks

speaker: KING
text: And changes fill the cup of alteration

speaker: KING
text: With divers liquors! O, if this were seen,

speaker: KING
text: The happiest youth, viewing his progress through,

speaker: KING
text: What perils past, what crosses to ensue,

speaker: KING
text: Would shut the book and sit him down and die.

speaker: KING
text: ’Tis not ten years gone

speaker: KING
text: Since Richard and Northumberland, great friends,

speaker: KING
text: Did feast together, and in two years after

speaker: KING
text: Were they at wars. It is but eight years since

speaker: KING
text: This Percy was the man nearest my soul,

speaker: KING
text: Who like a brother toiled in my affairs

speaker: KING
text: And laid his love and life under my foot,

speaker: KING
text: Yea, for my sake, even to the eyes of Richard

speaker: KING
text: Gave him defiance. But which of you was by—

speaker: KING
text:
To
                 
                Warwick
                .
                 
              You, cousin Nevil, as I may remember—

speaker: KING
text: When Richard, with his eye brimful of tears,

speaker: KING
text: Then checked and rated by Northumberland,

speaker: KING
text: Did speak these words, now proved a prophecy?

speaker: KING
text: “Northumberland, thou ladder by the which

speaker: KING
text: My cousin Bolingbroke ascends my throne”—

speaker: KING
text: Though then, God knows, I had no such intent,

speaker: KING
text: But that necessity so bowed the state

speaker: KING
text: That I and greatness were compelled to kiss—

speaker: KING
text: “The time shall come,” thus did he follow it,

speaker: KING
text: “The time will come that foul sin, gathering head,

speaker: KING
text: Shall break into corruption”—so went on,

speaker: KING
text: Foretelling this same time’s condition

speaker: KING
text: And the division of our amity.

speaker: WARWICK
text: There is a history in all men’s lives

speaker: WARWICK
text: Figuring the natures of the times deceased,

speaker: WARWICK
text: The which observed, a man may prophesy,

speaker: WARWICK
text: With a near aim, of the main chance of things

speaker: WARWICK
text: As yet not come to life, who in their seeds

speaker: WARWICK
text: And weak beginning lie intreasurèd.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Such things become the hatch and brood of time,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And by the necessary form of this,

speaker: WARWICK
text: King Richard might create a perfect guess

speaker: WARWICK
text: That great Northumberland, then false to him,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Would of that seed grow to a greater falseness,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Which should not find a ground to root upon

speaker: WARWICK
text: Unless on you.

speaker: KING
text: Are these things then necessities?

speaker: KING
text: Then let us meet them like necessities.

speaker: KING
text: And that same word even now cries out on us.

speaker: KING
text: They say the Bishop and Northumberland

speaker: KING
text: Are fifty thousand strong.

speaker: WARWICK
text: It cannot be, my lord.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Rumor doth double, like the voice and echo,

speaker: WARWICK
text: The numbers of the feared. Please it your Grace

speaker: WARWICK
text: To go to bed. Upon my soul, my lord,

speaker: WARWICK
text: The powers that you already have sent forth

speaker: WARWICK
text: Shall bring this prize in very easily.

speaker: WARWICK
text: To comfort you the more, I have received

speaker: WARWICK
text: A certain instance that Glendower is dead.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Your Majesty hath been this fortnight ill,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And these unseasoned hours perforce must add

speaker: WARWICK
text: Unto your sickness.

speaker: KING
text: I will take your counsel.

speaker: KING
text: And were these inward wars once out of hand,

speaker: KING
text: We would, dear lords, unto the Holy Land.
